data.title: PSQ Holdings appoints Michael Pena as CFO.& Treasurer
data.description:
• PSQ Holdings (NYSE: PSQH) said on Tuesday that Michael Pena, current SVP of Finance, had been appointed CFO and Treasurer, effective May 1, 2026, while James Rinn had resigned as CFO, effective April 30, 2026, and had agreed to remain a Class III Director.
  • Furthermore, Krista Wenzel had been named CAOr, effective May 1, 2026.
  • Pena brings experience across structured finance, credit, operations, investor relations, and financial technology and played a key role in Credova’s acquisition in March 2024.
  • He had previously served as Credova’s CFO and Director of Finance and had earlier held roles at State Street Bank and Trust.
  • Wenzel had in-depth knowledge of public company leadership, capital markets, and enterprise finance and had been serving as SVP of Finance & Accounting since November 2025.
  • She had previously held the role of VP of Accounting and had earlier served in CFO positions across infrastructure investment platforms at Meridiam.

data.title: MGP Ingredients to temporarily idle two Kentucky distilleries
data.description:
• MGP Ingredients (MGPI) will temporarily idle distilling at Limestone Branch Distillery and Lux Row Distillers starting May 1, 2026.
  • The move is aimed at aligning production with current inventory levels.
  • Distilling will continue at its main facility in Lawrenceburg Distillery to support ongoing demand.
  • Operations could resume in as early as 12 months, depending on inventory conditions.
  • The decision impacts 33 employees, with support being provided during the transition.
  • Warehousing, bottling, barrel programs, and visitor centers at both sites will remain fully operational.
  • Reaffirms full year 2026 financial outlook.

data.title: Wealthfront platform assets slip 2.1% M/M in March
data.description:
Wealthfront's (WLTH)  platform assets of $93.2B at March 31, 2026, fell 2.1% from Feb. 28, 2026, but rose 14.5% from March 31, 2025, the company said on Tuesday.

 Cash management assets of $45.5B edged up 0.5% M/M and rose 2.6% Y/Y, while investment advisory assets of $47.7B dipped 4.5% from February but climbed 28.8% from March 2025.

Total funded clients of 1.44M increased 1.0% M/M and 15.8% Y/Y.

Total net deposits of $596M increased from $271M as of Feb. 28, 2026, and dropped from $1.20B at March 31, 2025.

Wealthfront (WLTH) started trading publicly on Dec. 12, 2025.

data.title: Algoma Steel surges after forming partnership for ballistic steel production
data.description:
Algoma Steel (ASTL) rallied into the close Tuesday, finishing up 5.2%, after saying it formed a joint venture with Canadian defense manufacturer of smart armored vehicles Roshel to establish a Canadian center for ballistic steel production.

By advancing a made-in-Canada approach to critical defense inputs, the companies said the Roshel Algoma Defence JV will enable new, sovereign production of ballistic steel solutions to support defense procurements, including for light utility vehicles, the Domestic Arctic Mobility Enhancement program, ships, submarines, and broader Canadian defense needs.

The partnership also will support the use of ballistic steel across other industries, including infrastructure, marine, aerospace, and security platforms, while creating opportunities to export Canadian-made ballistic steel solutions to allied countries, the companies said.

Ballistic steel is a special type of relatively lightweight, hardened steel that protects against blasts or bullets.

Algoma Steel (ASTL) "is ready to enhance Canada's defense capacity, starting now," CEO Rajat Marwah said. "This partnership with Roshel puts our production capabilities, our workforce, and our Sault Ste. Marie facilities directly in service of Canada's national security."

data.title: Greenbrier Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47
data.description:
• Greenbrier press release (GBX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47.
  •  Strong operating cash flow of $159 million.
  •  Board increased quarterly dividend by 6% to $0.34 per share, representing Greenbrier’s 48th consecutive quarterly dividend.

data.title: illumin Holdings appoints Tal Hayek CEO following leadership exit
data.description:
• illumin Holdings (ILLM:CA) announced that CEO Simon Cairns stepped down to pursue other opportunities.
  • The company appointed co-founder Tal Hayek as the new CEO, effective April 7, 2026, as he returns to lead the company and drive its journey advertising platform.
  • Stock traded at $0.57, up 2.51% during regular trading hours.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58, revenue of $222.4M
data.description:
• Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. press release (PXED): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58.
  • Revenue of $222.4M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA was $34.8 million for second quarter 2026, compared to $32.3 million for second quarter 2025, and adjusted diluted earnings per share was $0.58 for the second quarter 2026, compared to $0.56 for second quarter 2025.2

data.title: Levi Strauss & Co. CFGO Harmit Singh to retire, successor search begins
data.description:
• Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) announced on Tuesday that Executive Vice President and Chief Financial & Growth Officer (CFGO) Harmit Singh will continue in his role as CFGO until a successor is appointed and then transition to serve as Special Advisor, following which he will retire.
  • The company said it has initiated a comprehensive search process with the assistance of an executive search firm.
  • The company stated that Singh will continue to serve as CFGO until a successor is appointed and will remain for a planned transition as Special Advisor to ensure continuity.
  • Singh joined LS&Co. in 2013 as Chief Financial Officer, taking responsibility for the company’s global finance, information technology, M&A, investor relations, strategic sourcing, and global business services functions. In 2023, his role expanded to include chief growth officer, where he helped shape its corporate strategy, accelerate transformation initiatives, and advance several key enablers of our future, including global real estate, franchise expansion, and the development of our Global Talent Hubs. Before joining LS&Co., Singh served as chief financial officer at Hyatt Hotels Corporation and held division CFO roles at Yum! Restaurants International and Pizza Hut.
  • LEVI +6.03% after hours to $20.9. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Scorpio Tankers announces proposed offering of convertible senior notes
data.description:
• Scorpio Tankers (STNG) intends to offer $300M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering.
  • The company also expects to grant to the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $45M aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • The company expects to use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to repurchase shares of its common stock concurrently with the closing of the offering. 
  • The company intends to use a portion of the net proceeds from the offering to repurchase shares of common stock as described above and the remainder of the net proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • STNG +0.23% after hours to $74.36.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Levi Strauss Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42, revenue of $1.7B
data.description:
• Levi Strauss press release (LEVI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42.
  • Revenue of $1.7B.
  • Operating margin was 11.4% in Q1 2026 compared to 12.5% in Q1 2025. Adjusted EBIT margin was 12.5% in Q1 2026 compared to 13.4% in Q1 2025, reflecting the impact of tariffs and planned increases in advertising.
    • Gross margin was 61.9% compared to 62.1% in Q1 2025 primarily due to the impact of tariffs, partially offset by price increases and less promotional activity.
    • Selling, general and administrative (SG&A) expenses were $872 million compared to $749 million in Q1 2025. Adjusted SG&A expenses were up 15.7% to $861 million compared to $744 million last year primarily due to the planned higher advertising costs in connection with the launch of the Behind Every Original campaign, the higher-than-expected sales volume and foreign exchange.
  • Interest and other income (expense), net, which includes foreign exchange gains and losses, were income of $30 million and expenses of $15 million in the aggregate in Q1 2026 and Q1 2025, respectively.
  • The effective income tax rate was 22.4%, compared to 20.6% in Q1 2025.
  • Net income from continuing operations was $177 million compared to $140 million in Q1 2025. Adjusted net income was $167 million compared to $150 million in Q1 2025.
  • Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was $0.45 compared to $0.35 in Q1 2025. Adjusted diluted earnings per share was $0.42 compared to $0.38 in Q1 2025.
  •
